findings.message: New metric `app_frontend_raw_path_total` labels by the raw full path (including the query string) with no sanitization, no distinct-value cap, and no "other" overflow bucket. Every unique URL+query string becomes a new Prometheus series, so this label is unbounded / high-cardinality and will blow up the TSDB (the rest of the module is careful: routes/attribution/referrers all go through _bump_capped + _san).
findings.suggestion: Drop the query string and sanitize, then cap the distinct values with an overflow bucket like the other labels. Route the value through _san(route_without_query) and store via _bump_capped(self._raw_paths, value, self.MAX_ROUTES) so the label collapses to a fixed-size set with an "other" bucket. Better yet, reuse the existing bounded app_frontend_page_views_total route label and drop this series entirely.

findings.message: record_raw_path() stores the raw full_path (incl. query string) verbatim as a metric key with no cap or "other" overflow, the source of the unbounded cardinality emitted by app_frontend_raw_path_total. Unlike record_page_view, it bypasses _san and _bump_capped.
findings.suggestion: Strip the query string, sanitize with _san, and store via _bump_capped(self._raw_paths, value, self.MAX_ROUTES, overflow="other") so the series count stays bounded.
